heading: Protection for veterans preference employees
label: 593 Protection for veterans preference employees
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)59340:490c., , .Pub. L. 104–52, title V, § 503109 Stat. 491Nov. 19, 1995

children.heading: .—Covered services
children.label: 1 .—Covered services
children.text: The term “covered services” means any guard, elevator operator, messenger, or custodial services.

children.heading: .—Sheltered workshop
children.label: 2 .—Sheltered workshop
children.text: The term “sheltered workshop” means a sheltered workshop employing the severely handicapped under chapter 85 of title 41.

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: b .—In General
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (c), amounts made available to the General Services Administration pursuant to may not be obligated or expended to procure covered services by contract if an employee who was a permanent veterans preference employee of the Administration on , would be terminated as a result.section 592 of this titleNovember 19, 1995

children.heading: .—Exception
children.label: c .—Exception
children.text: Amounts made available to the Administration pursuant to may be obligated and expended to procure covered services by contract with a sheltered workshop or, if sheltered workshops decline to contract for the provision of covered services, by competitive contract for a period of no longer than 5 years. When a competitive contract expires, or is terminated for any reason, the Administration shall again offer to procure the covered services by contract with a sheltered workshop before procuring the covered services by competitive contract.section 592 of this title
